script_foreign: You are right. You are not right.
He is tired and hungry. He is not tired or hungry.
script_korean: 그는 피곤하고 배고파. 그는 피곤하지도 배고프지도 않아.

script_foreign: She is very gorgeous. She's not very gorgeous.
script_korean: 그녀는 매우 아름다워. 그녀는 그다지 아름답지 않아.

script_foreign: My father is very exhausted today.
This is a very expensive bicycle.
The weather isn't very nice today.
Hi. My name is John. I'm 20.
script_korean: 안녕, 내 이름은 존이야. 나는 20 살이야.

script_foreign: Hello. I am Sarah. This is Jenny.
Jenny is my best friend. We are very close.
script_korean: 제니는 내 제일 친한 친구야. 우린 진짜 친해.

script_foreign: Be quiet, please. You are too loud.
Please be careful. It is dangerous.
Yes, you are late. No, you aren't late.
script_korean: 그래, 너 늦었어. 아니, 너 안 늦었어.

script_foreign: Yes, I am nervous now. No, I'm not nervous now.
script_korean: 응, 나 지금 긴장 돼. 아니, 나 지금 긴장 안 돼.

script_foreign: Yes, I am free now. No, I'm not free now.
script_korean: 어, 나 지금 시간 돼. 아니, 나 지금 시간 안돼.

script_foreign: Yes, he is. No, he is not.
Yes, they are. No, they are not.
Isn't the food here pretty good?
Isn't K-pop pretty popular these days?
script_korean: 요즘 케이팝이 꽤 인기 있지 않아?

script_foreign: Is the weather freezing cold outside?
Hello, I'm Sarah. Is John here?
Aren't you guys close? You and Jenny are best friends.
script_korean: 너희들 가까운 사이 아니야? 너랑 제니는 절친한 친구잖아.

script_foreign: I was young. I was not young.
I was tired this morning. I wasn't tired this morning.
script_korean: 난 오늘 아침에 피곤했어. 난 오늘 아침에 피곤하지 않았어.

script_foreign: You were wrong this time. You weren't wrong this time.
script_korean: 너 이번엔 틀렸었어. 너 이번엔 틀리지 않았었어.

script_foreign: He was kind and warm. He wasn't kind or warm.
script_korean: 그는 친절하고 따뜻했어. 그는 친절하지도 따뜻하지도 않았어.

script_foreign: She was gorgeous tonight. She wasn't gorgeous tonight.
script_korean: 그녀는 오늘밤 우아했어. 그녀는 오늘 밤 우아하지는 않았어.

script_foreign: His boss was very scary last night.
This was a really boring movie.
Those American TV shows were my favorites.
script_korean: 그 미국 tv 쇼들은 내가 가장 좋아하는 것들이었어.

script_foreign: That wasn't my favorite Netflix series.
script_korean: 그것은 내가 가장 좋아하는 넷플릭스 시리즈가 아니었어.

script_foreign: The weather was scorching hot yesterday.
His name was John. He was in his 30s.
I was in my 20s. I was still very young.
John was my close friend. John and I were best friends.
script_korean: 존은 내 가까운 친구였어. 존과 나는 절친한 친구였어.

script_foreign: Yes, you were mean to me. No, you weren't mean to me.
script_korean: 응, 너 나에게 짓궂었어. 아니, 너 나한테 안 짓궂었어.

script_foreign: Yes, I was different back then. No, I wasn't different back then.
script_korean: 응, 나 예전에는 달랐어. 아니, 나 예전에도 안 달랐어.

script_foreign: Was he here a while ago?
Yes, he was here. No, he wasn't here.
script_korean: 응, 그는 여기 있었어. 아니, 그는 여기 없었어.

script_foreign: Wasn't she there two hours ago?
Yes, she was here. No, she wasn't here.
script_korean: 응, 그녀는 여기 있었어. 아니, 그녀는 여기 없었어.

script_foreign: Weren't they sick two weeks ago?
Yes, they were. No, they weren't.
Wasn't this impossible back in the day?
Was this easy ten years ago?
Wasn't that pretty difficult in the past?
Was the weather hot and humid over there?
John wasn't here. Was Sarah there?
script_korean: 존은 여기 없었어. 사라는 거기 있었어?

script_foreign: Weren't you guys best friends back then?
I study English. I don't study English.
script_korean: 난 영어를 공부해. 나는 영어를 공부하지 않아.

script_foreign: You eat very fast. You don't eat very fast.
script_korean: 너 매우 빨리 먹는다. 너 매우 빨리 먹지 않는다.

script_foreign: He sings very well. He doesn't sing very well.
script_korean: 그는 노래를 매우 잘 해. 그는 노래를 매우 잘하지 않아.

script_foreign: She speaks English fluently. She doesn't speak English fluently.
script_korean: 그녀는 영어를 유창하게 해. 그녀는 영어를 유창하게 하지 않아.

script_foreign: Your friend doesn't like me so much.
script_korean: 니 친구는 나를 그렇게 좋아하진 않아.

script_foreign: Her boyfriend doesn't call her every day.
script_korean: 그녀의 남자친구는 그녀에게 매일 전화하지 않아.

script_foreign: Their friends watch a movie every night.
script_korean: 그들의 친구들은 매일 밤 영화를 봐.

script_foreign: This coffee shop always opens at 5 o 'clock.
These stores don't normally close at 9 o 'clock.
That movie starts soon. Let's watch it.
Those restaurants have bad reviews. Please don't go.
script_korean: 저 음식점들은 리뷰가 좋지 않아. 가지마.

script_foreign: Don't open the window. Just turn on the air conditioner.
script_korean: 창문을 열지 마. 그냥 에어컨을 켜.

script_foreign: Please have a seat and enjoy your meal.
